# @zakkster/lite-headless/rating

Star/icon rating input. Used for reviews, feedback, satisfaction
scores. Supports configurable item count, half-step values, hover
preview, keyboard nav, read-only mode, and clearable on re-click.

## API

```js
import { createRating } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/rating";

const rating = createRating({
    max: 5,
    defaultValue: 0,
    step: 1,                          // 1 (whole-star) or 0.5 (half-star)
    readOnly: false,
    clearable: false,                 // click selected item to zero
    onValueChange: (next, prev, reason) => {},
    onHoverChange: (hover) => {},
    ariaLabel: "Rating",
});
```

## Reactive accessors

```js
rating.value()         // committed value
rating.hoverValue()    // null if not hovering, else the hover value
rating.displayValue()  // hoverValue when hovering, else value
rating.isReadOnly()
```

## Mutations

```js
rating.setValue(v, reason)
rating.setHoverValue(v | null)
rating.clear()                  // setValue(0)
rating.setReadOnly(b)
```

## Attach

```js
rating.attachRoot(el)           // role=radiogroup; reactive data-value/display-value/max/step
rating.attachItem(el, index)    // role=radio; click + hover handlers; data-filled/data-half-filled/data-empty
rating.attachRail(el)           // optional: pointer-x mapping for half-step drag
```

`index` is 1-based -- the i-th star takes index `i`.

## Painted attributes

### Root (attachRoot)
- `role="radiogroup"`, `aria-label`
- `data-max`, `data-step`
- `data-value`, `data-display-value`
- `data-read-only`, `data-hovering` (when hover state present)

### Items (attachItem)
- `role="radio"`, `aria-checked="true|false"`
- `data-index`
- `data-filled` (displayValue >= index)
- `data-half-filled` (displayValue between (index - step) and index, exclusive)
- `data-empty` (otherwise)
- `tabindex="0"` for the focused item (`Math.ceil(value)` or 1 if value=0)

## Keyboard

| Key            | Effect                              |
|----------------|-------------------------------------|
| ArrowRight/Up  | +step                               |
| ArrowLeft/Down | -step                               |
| Home / End     | 0 / max                             |
| 1-9            | Set value directly (if <= max)      |

Keyboard listener is on the root, so any item can have focus and the
arrow keys work. Blocked in read-only mode.

## Custom element

```html
<lite-rating value="3" max="5">
    <button data-rating-item="1" aria-label="1 star">★</button>
    <button data-rating-item="2" aria-label="2 stars">★</button>
    <button data-rating-item="3" aria-label="3 stars">★</button>
    <button data-rating-item="4" aria-label="4 stars">★</button>
    <button data-rating-item="5" aria-label="5 stars">★</button>
</lite-rating>
```

### Host attributes
- `value="<num>"` (reactive)
- `max="<int>"`
- `step="1" | "0.5"`
- `read-only` (reactive)
- `clearable`
- `aria-label="..."`

### Host API
```js
host.ratingValue / .displayValue / .isReadOnly
host.setValue(v) / .clear() / .setReadOnly(b)
host._ratingInstance
```

### Custom element events
- `valuechange` `{ detail: { value, previousValue, reason } }`

## Half-step pattern

For half-star ratings, set `step: 0.5` and either:

1. **Per-item halves**: render each star as two clickable halves
   (left half attaches index - 0.5, right attaches index). Slightly
   more DOM but enables click-precision.
2. **Rail-based**: render whole-star items, but attach a parent
   `[data-rating-rail]` so the pointer-x position is mapped to a
   half-step value during hover/drag. Less DOM, less precise on
   touch, but smoother on mouse.

The primitive supports both -- pick whichever matches your design.

## Composition

- **Feedback module**: rating + form-field for label/error + textarea
  for "tell us more". Combine into a single submit handler.
- **Read-only display**: in a product list, show average ratings via
  `read-only` flag.
- **Mood picker** (instead of stars): pass custom icons in the item
  buttons; the primitive doesn't care what's inside the items.

## Notes

- `setHoverValue` is blocked in read-only mode (the primitive enforces
  this for both API and pointer paths so read-only is truly read-only).
- The default `tabindex` strategy puts focus on the currently-selected
  item (or item 1 if none selected). This is the standard radiogroup
  pattern -- Tab into the group lands on the current selection, then
  arrow keys move within.
- `step` must be exactly 1 or 0.5 -- the primitive throws on other
  values to prevent subtle snap bugs.

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
